desc:
Further down you continue, sometimes in a gradual, almost boring fashion, but at
other times in dangerous, precipitous descents. You pass a gargantuan boulder
that is lodged into the side of the mountain, and wonder, like hundreds of
travellers before, what on Arda could have put it there.
name: Broken Mountains

desc:
The fog rises up along the sides of this cold, enormous slab of granite and
shale, mingling with the steam of your exhalations, and confounding your vision.
All is dark and grey, dead and unmoving. Here and there a hardy, gnarled little
tree hangs from an unlikely perch, but besides that, all is lifeless. The
rubble-strewn little trail leads down the mountainside, into some barren hills
far below.
name: Broken Mountains

desc:
This small trail grows very steep, climbing up into the mountains to the south.
The ground gets rockier and more treacherous, as bits of loose shale and stone
shift under your feet. A multitude of other footsteps have been here to pack
down the rocky ground, but still it has not settled. A faint odour catches your
attention, and draws your eyes to a small glimpse of white bones.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
You have travelled long and far to reach this mighty set of mountains, the
Hithaeglir. The trail has been steep and cruel, yet you have persevered. Now,
instead of a majestic panorama or scenic beauty, a greyish mist rises up from
the cold, grey stone sides of the mountains and blocks the sights of the world
beyond. And, as this is but the start of the Misty Mountains, your tribulations
have only just begun.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You wander aimlessly in these treacherous mountains. The mist is a bit thinner
than it usually is and you can catch glimpses of the forsaken lands spread out
to your north. To the east, your climb gets much steeper as the mountains rise
up to a high pass.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The rocks on either side of this pass are wet and slimy to touch. The pass is a
bit worn as if many animals have passed this way. There are blood stains on some
of the rocks near you, making you wonder what kind of a fierce battle took place
here. You can descend from this place to the east and the west.
name: A Narrow Pass

desc:
A darkness settles on your heart as you wander through these barren mountains.
All around you is nothing but the cold stone that makes these mountains and the
mist that gives them their name. West of here, the mountains rise up slightly
into a high pass. It is too misty to see anything else.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Terrible thoughts fill your mind as you linger in this terrible place. Thick
cold mist is all around you. You can't tell which direction the mountains
continue in. Will going north lead you to safety? Is there safety anywhere in
these mountains? Something furry brushes your foot as you walk along but when
you look down you see nothing. It seems that you are being watched from every
direction.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains stretch out around you, rising majestically into the sky. Away to
the north, the spur upon which you stand reaches an abrupt end, at a what
appears from here to be a tall cliff. Perhaps from there you will be able to see
the countryside far about.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You are walking along the northern edge of this range of the Misty Mountains.
For some odd reason, you are overcome with pride to be alive in an area where
countless innocent creatures have doubtlessly suffered terrible deaths. What a
shame it would be if you died in these distant and barren lands.
name: Edge of the Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains suddenly get much steeper here. The climb looks easy but it will
be hard. You should have a good view from up above if this fog around you clears
up. Every once in a while, you imagine that you can hear screams in the distance
and the fog sometimes seems to be alive.
name: A Rising Mountain Slope

desc:
The fog that is south and east of you has thinned and you can peak out at the
lands below you. To the north and west are steep cliffs. Looking out northwest
from here, you can see the remains of a forest. Past the forest you think you
can see more mountains. It's hard to tell because the air is still a bit misty.
name: Overlook High in the Mountains

desc:
Walking across these raped plains is wearing you out. There is nothing to see
but burned and dead land all around you. To your east, you can enter the charred
hills...more raped land. The plains seem to continue endlessly to the north and
the west. South of you, you can enter the forest.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
The plains have risen up in elevation, but you are not quite in the Burned
Hills. This certainly is an evil place where many have died and none have cared.
To your north and west, the Burned Plains spread out before you. Heading east
will take you into the hills.
name: Entering the Burned Hills

desc:
This section of the hills is littered with burnt wood and corpses. An awful
smell seeps from the ground and burns your nose when you breathe. Maybe this was
a graveyard? From the way it smells, it certainly could have been. The ground
here looks strange, like there is something buried in it...
name: Burned Hills

desc:
The trail has become steep here, forcing you to travel slowly lest you lose your
footing. Letting your gaze travel south, further down the trail to the
mountains, the sky seems so grey that the skyline is hardly discernible. The
tracks seem fainter here, due to the increase of rock in the soil.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
Twisting west and north, the tracks start following a ridge that beings to rise
above the surrounding lands. The grey of the rocks and earth grows dirtier and
deader to the southwest, and the air gets chill from the altitude that its
climbs to. A frigid wind blows through, whipping the loose tails of your
clothing.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
Oh no!! As you stupidly attempt to climb down the cliff wall, you lose your grip
and fall. Down, down, down you go until finally you SPLAT on the hard rocks. Oh
well, so much for your puny little life! Maybe next time you'll look first.
name: Falling...

desc:
At last you are out of that wasted place. Where to go from here, though. It
looks like your only options are to go back into the forest or to head north to
some desolate looking plains. The plains look more inviting from here as you
consider descending into the forest again.
name: A Way through the Burned Forest

desc:
It's amazing how much good some fresh air does you. This hill rises out of the
forest of burned trees below you. The hill itself is completely bare, but at
least its not covered with the charred remains of trees and who knows what else.
You had better get ready to brave the forest to your south because it is the
only way out of here.
name: A Hill above the Burned Forest

desc:
Wow, you sure are getting sick of walking around this depressing place. If only
you could get up away from the forest floor for just a moment. Wait, to the
north it looks like there's a small hill. Maybe if you can clamber up it, your
claustrophobia will be eased temporarily.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
The corpses of burnt trees are all around you. What could have started a fire
powerful enough to clean this whole land? Everything around you looks more or
less the same, but you think you might be able to get out of here by heading
upwards. It looks like you can also head down and deeper into the forest.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
Somehow you have descended even further from the forest floor to reach this evil
place. There are no burned trees here like there are where you just came from,
but you almost wish there were. This place is dark and barren except for a few
strange plants. It makes your skin crawl.
name: Under the Burned Forest

desc:
You are now in a small space between the rolling hills. You are still in the
hills, but you are at a lower elevation than your were before. South of you are
the Misty Mountains. The hills continue to the north and the west between the
high mountains.
name: Dip in the Burned Hills

desc:
The small trickle of water that runs down from the mountains must have been much
larger at one time to have carved such a deep ravine. It is too puny to even
drink from. The steep walls of the ravine and the crack that it becomes as it
continues north make your only exit back the way you came. Yes, more climbing,
more mountains, and more fear.
name: Deep Ravine

desc:
Up into the mountains you go again. You have the strange feeling that you have
been here before. The mountains continue to the east, west, and south of you. To
the east they rise high into the mist until you can no longer see them. To the
west and south, they go on into the distance.
name: Into the Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains drop a bit in elevation just to the west of here. You can easily
climb down the mountain side to where the air is thinner and your head will spin
less. You can see nothing in the other directions because of the thick fog that
gave these mountains their name.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Hiking in these mountains makes you wonder if there is good anywhere in the
world. You are beginning to jump at shadows, and you can't help but wonder if
the next corner you round will reveal a clan of trolls or a wolf pack. The mist
is thick, keeping you from viewing the lands that surround you and making you
paranoid.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Picking a path carefully where none before existed, you are unable to tell if
you are making any progress. The mountains continue to all look the same,
covered as they are with a thick fog. The fog sticks to your clothes and the
clamminess works its way to your soul.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The crazy shape of these mountains at times leaves you in a place where the only
way out is to go back the way you came. This is such a place. After countless
hours wandering in the Misty Mountains, you have again hit a dead end. For some
reason the word 'dead' seems to resonate in your mind and in your soul.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
What terrible force of nature made this place? The mountains here are barren and
colourless. In the distance, wolves can be heard howling wildly. How can even
wolves survive in this lonely place? What do they live off of? What do they eat?
What might they eat!?
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The road takes a turn here to the west. It leads deeper south and maybe into
some unknown regions of the mountains. When you look towards the top of the
mountains, all you see is fog everywhere which hides most of the mountains.
Occasionally you hear the sound of great big eagles high up in the sky. You
wonder what kind of magic is hidden in the niches of these mountains.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
The road passes through a narrow gully here, sharply turning from east to south.
Piled in the middle of the trail are the bodies of two unfortunate travellers --
elven hunters perhaps -- who look to have succumbed to an ambush of some sort.
The rocky sides of the gully could easily hide dozens of un- seen enemies.
Whoever these travellers were, they gave a good accounting of themselves -- the
presence of several goblin corpses attests to this.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
To the south, the road slopes downward through a narrow gap between two large
mountains. In that direction, the mountains look to give way eventually to rocky
hills. To the north, the road slants upward, again following the only path
between the two peaks, before it makes a sharp turn to the east and is lost from
view.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
The road continues south from here. To your west you see a small cutout in the
mountain side which allows you to step out of the road into comparative secrecy
away from the eyes of other curious travellers. The cold is beginning to get to
you and you wish you had more clothes and some ale to warm yourself with.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
The narrow road seemingly ends here, as its route south quickly becomes lost
among some low hills. To the west is a deep valley filled with a dark pine
forest. Occasional lonely howls echo up from the ominous forest, but the trees
are too dense to locate their source.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
The hills are little more than jagged outcroppings of rock thrusting upward
through the earth. On all sides, the high peaks of the Misty Mountains rise,
enclosing this oasis of rocky hills. There is a road to the north that once led
through this area, but its path has become lost so there is no indication of a
correct direction to proceed.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The rocky hills continue in all directions, until they eventually give way to
mountains once more. These hills are like slanted rock tables, all tilted up
from west to east at steep angles. The sharp rocks and rocky soil make walk- ing
difficult, threatening to shred even the toughest of boots. Some distance to the
east, there is a hill noticeably larger and of different appearance than the
others.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
In every direction save east steep cliffs squelch all hope of movement. To the
west, these rocky hills continue, bare except for occasional thorny plants that
seem more dead than alive.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
These hills are essentially large blocks of granite that have thrust upward
through the tortured earth. On all sides, the high peaks of Hithaeglir rise,
totally enclosing these barren hills. To the east, an especially large hill
dominates the scenery.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
This hill, though smaller than the mountains to the east and south, is still
very impressive. It is crowned by a small circle of stones, probably the remnant
of some ancient tower -- perhaps a road or trail once led through this area. In
every direction save west, the hillside is too steep and dangerous to attempt to
negotiate, and any attempt to do so would surely send one sprawling to one's
death on the jagged rocks below.
name: Circle of Stones

desc:
These barren hills are formed by sharp rectangular blocks of granite that have
been tilted at angles by geological forces. There is very little vegetation --
nothing larger than a few scraggly thornbushes has successfully found purchase
in the scant soil. To the southeast, an especially large hill rises above the
others.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
To the east is the the exit from this underground chamber. The walls of this
place exude a sense of age -- perhaps even before the Witchking of Angmar first
made his power felt and fell things gathered from all corners of Arda.
name: Crumbling Passage

desc:
The walls here are in better shape than elsewhere, and some faint carvings in an
unknown language can be made out high on the western wall. This age of this
place is an almost tangible force, seeming to mock those who dare invade with
their own insignificance in the grand pageant of time.
name: Crumbling Passage

desc:
The purpose behind this place is unclear. The walls are lined with ancient
masonry that crumbles at the slightest touch, indicating great age. Perhaps this
hill once housed a tower for guarding the dangerous road that led to these rocky
hills, or perhaps this place is the tomb of some ancient and forgotten king.
name: Crumbling Passage

desc:
This entire hill seems to be hollow, an amazing accomplishment considering the
solid granite exterior. The purpose of this place has been lost in antiquity.
Maybe this was once a watchtower of old Angmar, or perhaps this place predates
even the Witchking's rule.
name: Beneath the Hill

desc:
The valley slopes steeply upward to the east, eventually meeting a trail that
runs along the top of a ridge. The trees, mainly pine and fir, are old, perhaps
old enough to remember the icy winters of the Witch-king's rule over Angmar to
the north. The valley floor lies to the west, enclosed on three sides by steep
mountain slopes.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
A rock cliff looms to the north, and the multitude of large boulders scattered
among the ancient trees hints at frequent rockslides. The ground here is covered
with old pine-needles and the tracks of many wolves. Howling and occasional
sounds of padding feet just out of sight heightens the sense of danger here.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
There is a large notch in the cliff directly to the north, and many boulders
have been piled there to obscure whatever lies within it. The ancient pines and
firs are dense here, their black trunks giving this area a gloomy and foreboding
atmosphere.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
The deep valley comes to an end here -- it is impossible to head farther west or
north. To the south, more of the forest stretches, but in that direction as well
a cliff is visible. The forest is dark -- very little sunlight can filter down
through the thick canopy of pine needles -- and the howling of wolves indicates
that this area may not be a good place to tarry.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
There is an ominous cave to the south, nestled in this corner of the valley. The
ground is littered with bones here -- mostly of elk and other large game, but
there are a few of unmistakably humanoid origin. To the west and above the cave
to the south rise sheer cliffs, inaccessible to those without wings.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
The black trunks of the ancient pines press close together, forcing one to move
via circuitous routes through this area. In a small clearing lies the carcass of
a large elk -- almost completely stripped of meat, but obviously no more than a
few days old. Its spine had been crushed and shattered by some mighty blow. Some
of the smaller bones have been scattered about -- probably by scavenging wolves.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
The trees thin out a bit here, on the crest of a small hill in the valley floor.
Immediately to the east and south, the hard grey granite of the moun- tains
rises abruptly in a sheer rocky cliff. To the west and north the dense pine
forest hides the ground, and also wolves judging by the occasional howls that
can be heard in the wind.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
This cave is fairly dry, and its floor is made of solid granite with a few old
pine needles strewn about -- probably blown in from the forest to the north. The
cave extends further into the rock to the east, and from that direction the
smell of rotting meat can be detected.
name: Dry Cave

desc:
This dark cave smells of unwashed bodies and rotting meat. There are three piles
of greasy pine needles and moss that look like crude beds, and scattered about
are remnants of past meals. The cave continues to the south, but the darkness
prevents any details from being espied.
name: Sleeping Cave

desc:
The cave dead-ends here, becoming too narrow to pass any further. This part of
the cave seems to be used as a refuse room -- with many gnawed bones, some still
with clinging pieces of meat, tossed carelessly about. The smell also hints that
this area is used as a sewer, as the trolls seem to be content to live in their
own filth.
name: Back of Cave

desc:
This area is the intersection of two major tunnels -- one passage leads to the
west, while another slopes steeply down from north to south. This en- tire area
is littered with the nasty leavings of goblins -- everything from feces to bones
covered with clinging bits of rotting meat has been strewn haphazardly about the
area.
name: Confluence of Tunnels

desc:
This narrow tunnel is quite steep, going down as it goes north. Its walls are of
black rock, and no time has been spared to finish their rough edges. Only the
occasional gnawed bone litters the stone floor. The air is smoky and you feel a
warm breeze coming from below.
name: A Steep Passage

desc:
Six flickering torches are mounted on one side of this roughly circular chamber,
on either side of the passage entrance. Their baleful light reveals a scene from
Hell. The walls and ceiling are made of dark, rough-hewn rock and the floor is
covered with a stinking litter. In the deep gloom on the far side of the
chamber, an altar to darkness rises among the smoke like a throne to Morgoth or
a remembrance of long-fallen Thangorodrim.
name: Torchlit Chamber

desc:
As you go deeper into these caves, signs of goblin habitat increase. There are a
few faint carvings on the wall of the cavern, showing goblins worshipping their
leaders. Other carvings show the leaders moving towards the interior followed by
slaves carrying large chests.
name: Inner Caves

desc:
This seems like the innermost cave. There is a huge goblin chair in the middle
and several small chairs to the side. On the walls there are carvings showing
previous goblin chieftains and innumerable others serving them. If one were to
study the carvings carefully, it would become apparent that some of those
displayed in the carvings are orcs.
name: Deep Chamber

desc:
This chamber seems to be on the path of an occasional underground river as it is
more damp than expected. The floor is largely buried in a soil made of gravel
and foul refuse. The air is humid and heavy with the smell of waste. An opening
in the ceiling shows where the waste comes from. The wall are covered by a thick
layer of moss and mildew, and numerous mushrooms grow on the dirt.
name: A Damp Cave

desc:
This cavern is very small and narrow. Those with claustrophobia will tend to
flee through the only visible exit towards the east. The ground is littered with
feces, some fresh, others old. There are bones and rotting carcasses at the rear
of the cavern. The most disgusting of the carcasses bear fresh teeth marks.
name: Inner Caves

desc:
This is a very large cavern with carved benches and other man made features. It
becomes apparent from the filthy clothes and other signs that these caves are
the home of the Goblins and were recently occupied. No wonder these caves smell
as they do.
name: Inner Caves

desc:
This portion of the inner caves is very cold and damp. The absence of all wild
animals and an abundance of deer, elk, and elf bones on the floor of the cavern
gives an appearance of someone's or something's feasting room. The dark corners
of the cave hide all signs of life that may lurk to the north or east. If
possible, a short rest before going ahead would be most advisable.
name: Inner Caves

desc:
This small chamber has oddly smooth and regular walls. They make the same
precise angle at every corner. In the centre of the floor, a windlass has been
set up over a large black hole. A rope disappears into the hole.
name: Well Chamber

desc:
The inner caves are dark, cold and clammy, not at all comfortable for those who
are used to living in valleys and mountains. But in this locale, this may be the
best available dwelling. Shelter from curious eyes as well as the opportunity to
light a fire without having to deal with fierce winds are definitely a plus.
There are signs of local inhabitants, who have grown accustomed to living in
damp caves.
name: Inner Caves

desc:
The tunnel here is much wider than those to the north. A boulder to the west has
become a crude door that has been used from time to time to seal off the inner
caverns. What is not obvious is if this door was meant to keep intruders out of
the caverns or to keep something in the cavern.
name: Under the Mountains

desc:
The tunnels go east to south here. The darkness prevents you from seeing too
much of the tunnels. There might be hordes of goblins in them and yet you might
not see them. You can't even see your own feet.
name: Under the Mountains

desc:
This seems to be the deepest part of the tunnel. There is a puddle of water in
the middle of the room with a reddish tint. After examining the water it becomes
clear that elves have recently been stewed here. Clearly this is not a place
friendly to elves and their friends. No sound can be heard from the tunnels, so
maybe the attackers are hiding in the darkness.
name: Under the Mountains

desc:
The tunnels extend on and on. Though the wind can be felt, its origin can not be
discerned. A strange whistling sound can be heard coming from above. At first,
this sound could be mistaken as artificial, but after careful examination of the
walls, it becomes obvious that the sound is merely the wind blowing through the
little holes in the wall.
name: Under the Mountains

desc:
The tunnel is very narrow. The sides are lined with jagged rocks that protrude
from the side. Through a crack in the boulders, a valley can be seen to the
south while the tunnel continues to the north. There is a powerful smell of
goblin to the north.
name: Backdoor

desc:
This small and dusty chamber seems to be well used -- the floor is covered with
the footprints of some type of humanoid. A small shelf in the north- ern wall
about two metres above the ground gives access to a short tunnel that slopes
steeply upward to a boulder that acts as a crude door. Another narrow tunnel
leads down to the south, deeper in to the hidden recesses of the mountain.
name: High Chamber

desc:
This seems like the lowest point of the mountain ranges in the vicinity. For a
change your lungs get a good supply of oxygen, making you feel lightheaded
suddenly. You find that the only direction you can go from here is west.
name: Small Valley

desc:
You see as you climb lower down the mountain that there is a small pass to your
east. It does not seem like a well frequented one. Maybe going that way will get
you to a road that does not go up dangerous mountain paths. There is what seems
like the remnants of a campfire here. Maybe someone was here not so long back.
You hope it is not one of the enemies.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
Going down the mountain, you feel a sense of gratitude towards the gods for
having kept you from death's clammy grip. Here you see that you can take a
variety of different routes. Your past experience in this land makes you wonder
which way would be the safest. Also you hope that once you choose a path, you
can retrace your steps easily in case of trouble.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
This narrow and rocky valley was once home to a mountain stream that has left an
easily passable trail as its legacy. The valley floor is littered with debris
from old rockslides -- one of which was probably responsible for the drying of
the stream -- and the sides are too steep to be negotiated.
name: Dry Streambed

desc:
This is the southern most tip of the mountain top. From here you can climb down
the mountain pretty easily. This side of the mountain seems much safer than the
others. Farther south you see the range continuing as far as your eyes can see.
The fog that envelopes the top seems to be pretty thin, though from far you
thought it was dense.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
This small overlook to the west of the trail affords a magnificent view of a
deep forested valley to the south. West and north are nothing but the cold grey
peaks of the Misty Mountains -- the tallest stretching thousands of metres above
this vantage point.
name: By the Road

desc:
The entrance is a small space and littered with rubble. Along the grey rock
walls stand benches and a few tables. The roof is jagged, with sharp edges
protruding in different directions. The cave is damp, and moss has spread across
the ceiling. Brown bottles and clay jars lie cracked open on the ground. A small
desk with a stool stands next to the entrance. A narrow tunnel burrows deeper
beneath the Misty Mountains to the east.
name: Mine Entrance

desc:
A tunnel that is nearly eight feet high and widest in the centre runs from north
to south. Its unlevel, earthen walls appear to have been carved out with a
chisel. Broken roots that had been clumsily removed from the tunnels passage
emerge from the ceiling, walls, and even the floor in some spots. The dirt is
severely disturbed along the passage's western edge. The southern entrance of
the tunnel is totally blocked by a rough grate that stretches from one side to
the other. A rusty gate is located in the middle of the grate.
name: Debris filled Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel has been significantly enlarged, and the cave walls have been
severely scraped. The underground floor has been pushed down by the stomping of
numerous feet and is heavily littered with chaotic piles of broken bones and
other debris. After the area was mined out, nothing appears to be left here.
name: Tunnel Intersection

desc:
Numerous torches have been set up in this part of the mine, and their light
casts eerie shadows on the rocks that are dispersed across the wet, roughed-up
walls. The northern side of the mine, where work has increased, has a large hole
dug into it. The additional mining is justified by the glint of the iron ore.
name: Hole in a Mine Tunnel

desc:
The ground gives place to a broad, dark corridor with a few towering stones and
a largely smooth rock floor. Although a big portion of this location may have
been a naturally occurring geological feature, it appears that the rock to the
south and east has been shattered and artificially molded into the shape of a
sizable tunnel.
name: Dark Corridor

desc:
Here, the great stone walls turn outward to create a truly enormous tunnel. The
ceiling is hidden by a shroud of shadow in the pitch-blackness above. The ground
is unexpectedly soft and appears to be composed of layers of gravel, sand, and
mud in the tunnel's centre. The walls slenderize toward the east, as though most
of the water had been absorbed into the bedrock.
name: Enormous Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is suitable for defence because it is large in the middle and gets
narrower to the north and south. The chances of holding this tunnel from
invaders have been improved by the installation of a sizable gate into the
northern rockwall.
name: Gate in a Rockwall

desc:
Torches have been hammered into the sidewalls of the narrow tunnel, casting
gyrating shadows on the surfaces. Several tiny bits of iron ore can be seen
among the rubble that blankets the ground. Other than that, there is nothing
down here but chilly and damp, sultry air.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel gradually ascends while going with the rock's grain. The dark reds
that stain the moist walls cause them to shine and glisten. Although the air is
musty and stale, it is chilly, odorless, and not particularly unpleasant. The
tunnel gets smaller as it moves north.
name: Glistening Tunnel

desc:
A lush valley, sprawling with low bushes and thin, scraggly trees, stretches to
the east and south. To the north, a narrow pass leads through the mountains in
that direction. To the south, the vegetation begins to thin as the ground
becomes more bare.
name: Bush-covered Valley

desc:
A narrow pass between tall mountains descends southerly here. The ground is
loose and gravelly, making passage hazardous and slippery. The mountain walls
funnel the cold wind here, creating strong gusts. The mountains on the east and
west create deep, dark shadows in the mountain pass.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
Mountainous terrain can be spotted in each direction. They appear to eventually
descend to flat land in the distance to the south and west, but continue to
climb higher to the east and north. The air here is cold and windy, with
occasional strong gusts. The terrain underfoot is gravelly, and pebbles and
small rocks tumble downward after each step.
name: Deep in the Mountains

desc:
The trail runs east and west here, stretching between a tall, steep, mountainous
wall to the south, and a slow-moving creek to the north. The trail appears to be
well-travelled, as it is dusty and cut significantly into the ground around it.
name: Valley Trail South of a Ravine

desc:
The valley here is wide, opening to the east and west, with a trail following it
in both directions. The trail is narrow, dusty, and gravelly, but it is worn and
well-travelled. A slow-moving creek borders the trail to the north, running
alongside it. Patches of short grasses and short, scraggly shrubs borders the
trail on either side.
name: Trail through a Valley

desc:
High walls of stone and dirt surround this dead end. Small, craggy trees
struggle to maintain life in this harsh environment. Tiny pebbles are scattered
across the ground, making footing slippery. Bird nests sit high up on flat
sections of rock. The mountain opens up to the west.
name: Mountain Dead End

desc:
The mountains are void of most plant life here; only some lichen grows on the
rock. The wind seems to be blowing constantly here, chilling the bones. The
mountains continue in all directions from here; to the south, they are
noticeably descending. While not easy, it's still possible to travel on this
stark mountain slope.
name: Stark Mountain Slope

desc:
It is clear from the few remaining tool marks that long ago there was a carved
pass here. Since that time the pass has become full of debris from various rock
and mud slides. It is no longer possible to even climb over the great mass of
fractured rock.
name: Debris Filled Pass

desc:
A wide, vast field rests at the feet of mountains surrounding it. The ground is
covered in a thick, green grass, and other vegetation and wildlife thrives here
as well. Tall, thin trees grow here and there, and the chirping of birds can be
heard. The air is cold and windy.
name: Thriving Valley

desc:
The mountains gradually climb to the east, while suddenly descending to the
south. The cold, blustery wind swirls here in the corner, as it gusts through
the mountain pass and valleys. To the south, a vast, green field can be spotted
north of a ravine and a creek.
name: Western end of a Narrow Pass

desc:
The Misty Mountains continue to stretch out across the land here. A large, flat
opening can be spotted to the south, but the southern slope makes travel in that
direction impossible. Caution should be taken when travelling through a mountain
pass that stretches east and west.
name: Narrow Pass

desc:
Tall mountains continue to stretch as far as the eye can see. A narrow mountain
pass runs east and west. To the north is a tall mountain with a smooth slope
that is impossible to traverse. To the south, a small cave opening can be seen
in the side of the mountain along the pass.
name: Narrow Pass

desc:
Cramped and damp, this cave has been naturally formed in the side of the
mountain, over the passage of time. The cave provides some shelter from the
harsh conditions outside, but the opening is just enough to allow the blustery
wind to blow inside. The walls and ceiling are covered with thin mosses and
lichen.
name: Miserable Cave

desc:
A steep slope on the side of a tall mountain, covered in snow and ice, climbs
upward into the sky toward the mountain summit. In the opposite direction,
several rocks and boulders protrude out from the mountainside, providing footing
along the treacherous traverse.
name: Steep Slope

desc:
Here is the summit of one mountain in an endless sea among the Misty Mountains.
Although it is lower than other summits, it still gives a great vantage of the
surrounding terrain. Snow-covered caps surround it in every direction, with
rocks and large boulders protruding here and there to create foot and hand holds
for dauntless travellers.
name: Craggy Summit in the Misty Mountains

desc:
High above the ground, this mountain is rocky and snow-covered. Footing is made
possible by jutting and jagged rocks that erupt from the sides of the mountain.
The northern slope is smooth and covered in a thick bed of snow. The air is
thin, cold, and damp.
name: Snow-covered Slope

desc:
Deep in the midst of the Misty Mountains, mountains of various sizes impede safe
travel in most directions. A valley opens to the south, while the tall mountains
continue to stretch west, north, and east. A narrow mountain pass is able to be
used to travel among the mountains here.
name: Mountains above a Valley

desc:
Rolling mountains stretch in every direction, and a small mountain pass can be
utilized to travel east and west. The passage is extremely treacherous and
cumbersome, hindering travel among the mountains and making it extremely slow.
Large rocks and boulders jut out of the mountain sides.
name: Rugged Mountains

desc:
Travel among the mountains comes to an abrupt ending here, as the northern,
eastern, and southern slopes of the mountain are extremely steep and hazardous.
Travel in any of those directions is nearly impossible, as falling would lead to
sure death. In the west, the mountains climb more gradual, providing much safer
passage.
name: Mountain Dead End

desc:
A narrow mountain ridge stretches east to west, along the crest of a tall
mountain. Passage here is dangerous, as the northern slope is almost a complete
drop to the ground far below. The mountain descends more gradually to the south
and east.
name: Windswept Ridge

desc:
A narrow mountain ridge results in a dead-end as it rises, preventing further
passage. A steep rise climbs to another ridge to the south that leads in a
southeasterly fashion. The craggy slopes of the mountains are covered in
moisture. Little vegetation grows between the boulders.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The Misty Mountains, vast and sprawling across the landscape, climb skyward in
every direction, as far as the eye can see. The slopes of the mountains are
rocky and craggy, and there are narrow passes between a few of the mountains,
allowing for travel among them.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The wind carries an odd smell to your freezing nose. You can't quite decide if
it's the smell of dead meat or trolls. Maybe it's both. You're deep in the
mountains now, and your only choice is to continue onward. The mist is so heavy
here that you won't see the trolls until you are on top of them if there are any
at all in this barren and cold place.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A welcome respite from the rocky, mountainous terrain, this valley floor is vast
and sprawling, teeming with vegetation and wildlife. Patches of grass, short
shrubs, and thin trees are spread throughout the area, and the rustling of
animals and chirping of birds can be heard throughout the valley.
name: Thriving Valley Floor

desc:
East of here, the mountains rise up so steeply that you can't even get a good
hold on the smooth rock. This part of the mountains seems somehow different and
you feel safe for the moment. The cold wind has stopped and the distant noises
that were probably screams have disappeared.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Mountains to the north and east, and a deep ravine to the south, create a
natural dead-end. Vegetation and wildlife flourish here, and the grass grows
thicker toward the west. Thorny bushes line the edges of the field around the
base of the mountains.
name: Thorny Corner

desc:
An obvious trail has been worn into the ground in the centre of the valley
floor. The valley is wide, and opens up to the east and west, with the trail
following it in those directions. There is a small creek here as well, which
flows slowly, and runs parallel to the trail.
name: Trail through a Wide Valley

desc:
The trail here stretches east and west, dusty, and gravelly underfoot. It is
mostly flat, with small, thin patches of short grasses bordering it on the north
and south. A calm, slowly-flowing stream runs parallel to the trail on its north
side. Just on the other side of the creek is a deep, narrow ravine.
name: Valley Trail South of a Ravine

desc:
A narrow trail runs east to west. The trail is rocky and dusty, worn in the
centre due to being well-travelled, with long grasses growing alongside it. To
the north of the trail, a slow-moving creek runs parallel to it. Shortly beyond
the north bank of the creek is a deep but narrow ravine, making travel beyond
impossible. To the south, steep mountains rise suddenly from the ground, and
there is a dark cave opening in the mountainside.
name: Trail before a Darkened Delve

desc:
Rows of stone blocks are stacked neatly and uniformly in a large circle, an
homage to a time long forgotten, the remnants of an ancient tower. In the centre
of the circle of stones is a short staircase also made of stone, crumbling and
broken. The first few steps of what appears to be a spiral staircase remain, but
not much else. There is a deep, narrow ravine to the west and south of the
ancient ruins, with no bottom to it in sight.
name: By a Ruined Tower

desc:
The slope of the mountain is composed of loose rocks and treacherous drops that
will lead to certain death if someone falls down. The Misty Mountains expand in
almost all directions from here. The mountain descends to the west, where a
valley floor is clearly visible from here. A trail that runs from the west to
the east is discernible in the south.
name: Treacherous Slope

desc:
The trail continues east to west, sloping down steeply to the west as it
descends into the mountainous terrain. The trail is dusty, and covered with
loose gravel, which shifts and tumbles downward when disturbed with a footstep.
Tall mountains stretch into the sky to the south, and traversing them does not
seem possible.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
The trail here ascends steeply upward to the east, a wall of mountainous rock
creating a natural border to its south. Small plants and grasses grow from the
ground beside the dusty trail, which also slopes downward westerly. The
mountainous surroundings open up to the north.
name: Trail through a Rising Valley

desc:
This is a bramble-filled valley floor near the Misty Mountains. The ground
ascends to the north, where the mountains loom ominously. Travelling in this
valley is not pleasant because of the thorny shrubs growing stubbornly in every
place, between the random boulders here and there.
name: Bramble-covered Valley Floor

desc:
The valley curves northward and eastward as the mountains open in each of those
directions. The surrounding mountains cast deep shadows on the valley, and the
air is damp and heavy. Mosses and lichen grow thick on the valley walls. A tall,
steep mountain stretches skyward to the south and west.
name: Large Valley

desc:
A valley runs in all directions but south, where a steep mountain wall blocks
passage in that direction. The valley is dark and damp, and mosses and lichen
cover the mountain walls on every side of the valley. The ground is bare and
rocky.
name: Valley

desc:
A low valley, surrounded by steep, mountainous walls, turns north and west. The
ground is rocky, making travel cumbersome. Mosses and lichen cover the walls.
Steep mountains rise in the east and south, making passage in those directions
impossible. The mountains cast deep shadows on the valley, and there is a
dampness in the air.
name: Valley Corner

desc:
This natural tunnel is wide in its centre and narrows to the north and south. A
large gate, made of iron, had once been installed in the tunnel's northern
rockwall, but it is now completely demolished. The tunnel to the south leads to
a crossroads.
name: Demolished Gate in a Rockwall

desc:
The tunnel goes mostly north to south with a fork to the east and has a smooth
rock floor. There's a chance that a sizable portion of this location was a
naturally occurring geological formation. The areas to the south and east,
however, appear to be former mine tunnels.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
At this juncture, the tunnel appears to be bigger. Although the earth below is
solid, it is covered in highly decomposed sludge. The tunnel generally runs from
north to south, with a branch that leads to an old mine tunnel to the east.
name: Intersection in a Tunnel

desc:
An almost eight-foot-high tunnel, widest in the centre, travels north to south.
Between the boulder-like walls and ceiling, roots progressively elongate. A
grate that originally blocked the tunnel's southern entrance has been totally
dismantled.
name: Dismantled Grate in a Tunnel

desc:
Little mounds of broken weapons and bones line the solid, unyielding earthen
walls of this cavern. A grate that originally acted as a barrier to the north
has been completely demolished. The corridor grows more stony behind the
destroyed grate, and the stench of scorched air is strong to the south.
name: Demolished Grate in a Cavern

desc:
This cavern's westernmost section is a dead-end with the only apparent entrance
to the east. Its walls are lined with cages that were once used to hold animals
before being destroyed. Whatever animals were here have long since disappeared
or perished.
name: Dead-end in a Cavern

desc:
The walls of this cavern's intersection are lined with burned muck. This area
still smells like death even though the fire that started the burning has long
since died out. The exit from this intersection that stands out the most is to
the east, where a larger cavern emerges.
name: Burnt Intersection in a Cavern

desc:
A deep pit occupies the majority of the cavern's floor space. The pit reeks of
death and is coated with charcoal. Broken bones and decaying parts of weapons
line the bottom of the pit. A tunnel of inky blackness leads north to an
intersection.
name: Deep Pit

desc:
This is the westernmost part of a vast cavern. The ceiling of the cave collapsed
toward the centre, making access to the deeper areas more difficult. The floor
is littered with splintered bones. The only evident way out of the cavern is to
the west.
name: Entrance to a Collapsed Cavern

desc:
There is no way through the rocks and earth here because the cavern has fully
collapsed; the only direction is to the west. Before it collapsed, the cavern
must have had a tall ceiling akin to a vault or a dome. Everything that was once
here has been buried beneath the debris.
name: Collapsed Cavern

desc:
The floor, the walls, and the ceiling of this portion of the cavern are all
completely burned. The scent of the charred remains permeates the air even
though the flames have long since died out. The cavern collapsed towards the
northeast, leaving only passages to the north and east.
name: Burned Cavern

desc:
The walls of the cavern are strewn with decaying wood. A massive stone rock is
set with bones in the centre of this cave. It smells bad since the floor is
covered in filth and decomposing animal fur. The large cavern collapsed toward
the southeast, leaving a lot of dirt and rocks on the ground.
name: Littered Cavern

desc:
There is no other way out than to the west because the cavern has collapsed
here. Rotten wood and rusted meat hooks can be found among the debris on the
floor. A sizable table was chiselled into the northern wall, which appears to be
the cave's natural wall. Animal and humanoid bones are scattered all over the
table.
name: Dead-End in a Large Cavern

desc:
The massive stone walls bend outward to form a very massive tunnel here. In the
pitch-blackness above, a curtain of shadow obscures the ceiling. In the tunnel's
centre, the earth is very soft and looks to be made up of layers of gravel,
sand, and mud. The walls thin out toward the east, as if the majority of the
water had been absorbed by the bedrock.
name: Massive Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel, which runs from west to south, is small and confined. The walls
rise to the ceiling above, and shelves stick out all along the way. Beds of
dried vegetation have been constructed for critters to sleep in. Animal feces
are strewn throughout the gravel that covers the stone floor.
name: Confined Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel gradually ascends while following the grain of the rock. The damp
walls sparkle and glisten because of the intense reds that stain them. Despite
being musty and stale, the air is cool, odourless, and not terribly unpleasant.
The tunnel narrows as it travels north.
name: Cold Tunnel

desc:
On the wet walls of this mine shaft, old torch holders have been set up. A big
hole has been excavated into the northern side of the mine. The mine runs
roughly in an east-west direction, with an intersection visible to the west.
name: Excavated Mine Tunnel

desc:
In a depression in the sloping floor of the cavern sits a burnt-out stone forge.
A dense coating of black dust surrounds the forge, indicating that a massive
fire completely destroyed this section of the cavern. The cavern's ceiling
collapsed to the north, leaving only a few feet of space between the rubble to
the north and the natural cavern's wall to the south.
name: Burnt-out Forge in a Collapsed Cavern

desc:
The floor of this cavern's far southern side is littered with wood debris that
has been partially burned into charcoal. The walls to the south are composed of
granite and have old scratch marks. The cavern's floor is covered in thick
layers of dust.
name: Littered Grounds in the Cavern

desc:
The cavern has a large, deep hole in the southeast corner. The bones that are
scattered throughout the hole suggest that it was formerly used for a grisly
purpose. Rotten and splintered wood on the ground appears to be the remnants of
some form of barricade, and the edges slope toward the north and west.
name: Dark Hole in the Cavern

desc:
This tunnel extends to the west until it becomes difficult to traverse due to a
lot of debris. Large rocks and earthen detritus were plainly brought up from the
collapsed ceiling. Only dust covers the floor, aside from occasional debris.
name: Dusty Grounds in a Cavern

desc:
Several decaying cages are fixed on the floor in the centre of the cavern. The
last intact chains and shackles from the cages, which show that they were used
to imprison humanoids, are left between little mounds of bones that are either
completely crushed or broken into tiny fragments.
name: Decaying Cages in the Cavern

desc:
The area next to the cavern's wall is littered with wooden debris and rusted
metal rings that appear to have been used as barrel hoops in the past. The
remains of an iron cauldron, now useless and filled with holes, are located
below an intact shaft that provides the cavern with fresh air.
name: Shaft in the Corner of a Cavern

desc:
Because of dirt and stone rubble left over after a collapse that blocked the
west and south entrances to the cave, the only visible exit is to the east. The
ruins of a stone well that had been built on a stone dais can be found in the
middle of this cavern's northern wall. The water that used to collect in the
well now trickles toward the south and disappears beneath debris.
name: Trickle in a Collapsed Cavern

desc:
This ruined kennel is in an awfully dilapidated state with rusty, twisted cages
and broken beams covering the floor. Rubble has fallen from the cavern ceiling
to the ground causing movement to be difficult. A few cages have been completely
flattened and rusty metal protrudes dangerously, ready to impale.
name: Ruinous Warg Kennel

desc:
Rubble has twisted and bent the crudely-built iron cages that line the walls of
this filthy cavern. Fallen rock can be seen everywhere among dusty piles of
droppings, gnawed bones, and litter. The entire cave stinks of excrement and
urine but even that can't distract by how the roof looks suspiciously unsafe and
ready to collapse at any given time.
name: Collapsed Warg Kennel

desc:
Simply calling these hills burned does not do them justice. You can't seem to
think of the right word as you wander through this ruined place. The hills go
east and west. To your north and south are the mighty Misty Mountains. You feel
slightly claustrophobic as you hurry on.
name: Burned Hills
